label: Intellihub Group - Product Engineer - EV Charging Hardware Cover Letter
subject: Application - Product Engineer
paragraphs:
I'm a Mechatronics Engineer, with a Masters Degree in Biomedical Engineering, a Diploma in Software Development, and a love for product developing. I have over eight years of
experience designing and engineering medical devices within the R&D, orthopaedic, and consulting space. This includes implant systems, prosthetics, surgical tools, and custombuilt manufacturing equipment. While my background is in healthcare, I'm an inventor at heart. In my spare time, I enjoy creating everything from mechanical tools to smart
software. Building and refining ideas is something I genuinely love.

My eight years of R&D product-development experience would transfer well to EVSE's Product Engineer role. Across 13+ projects, I have progressed products from concept through prototyping, testing, system integration, and regulatory readiness while solving mechanical, electrical, and software challenges. As a mechanical and product lead, I have coordinated cross-functional contributors, led design reviews, managed stakeholders, timelines, and budgets, and worked with suppliers, quality, manufacturing, and commercial teams. This background would support EVSE's development, testing, documentation, and improvement of reliable EV charging products.

I'm excited by the chance to explore something new, especially with a team that brings together engineering and user experience. I bring hands on design and prototyping
experience, a solid understanding of product lifecycles, and a practical approach to solving problems. I work best in collaborative environments where good design, smart
engineering, and real-world testing come together.
Thank you for considering my application. I'd love the opportunity to share more and see how I can contribute to your team.
